prd.oneLiner: Connect customer goals, roles, tasks, training, blockers, activation criteria, handoff, and outcome metrics.
prd.goal: Reduce time to core-feature activation for new customers by 25% and achieve 90% or more onboarding completion.
prd.whyNow: Adoption goals, customer-specific tasks, training, and blockers are split across email and CRM, increasing the risk that customers churn before realizing value.
prd.problem: Adoption goals, customer-specific tasks, training, and blockers are split across email and CRM, increasing the risk that customers churn before realizing value.
prd.solution: Connect customer goals, roles, tasks, training, blockers, activation criteria, handoff, and outcome metrics.
prd.alternatives: Email, chat, spreadsheets, and manual ledgers
prd.differentiator: Manage intake, processing, and operations data as one connected source of truth.

targets.name: New customer and customer-success team starting post-purchase adoption
targets.needs: Quickly register needed work and know its status.
targets.pain: Adoption goals, customer-specific tasks, training, and blockers are split across email and CRM, increasing the risk that customers churn before realizing value.

targets.name: Customer success and onboarding operations coordinator
targets.needs: Manage priority and history and review operations metrics.
targets.pain: Adoption goals, customer-specific tasks, training, and blockers are split across email and CRM, increasing the risk that customers churn before realizing value.

targets.name: System and policy administrator
targets.needs: Manage access, policy, and audit history.
targets.pain: Adoption goals, customer-specific tasks, training, and blockers are split across email and CRM, increasing the risk that customers churn before realizing value.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of operations requests completed on time

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Reduce time to core-feature activation for new customers by 25% and achieve 90% or more onboarding completion.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Customer account, onboarding goal, and task registration
Progress status, owner, blocker, and activation-history lookup
Kickoff, task assignment, training, review, activation, and handoff handling
Time-to-activation, completion, churn-risk, and onboarding-outcome reporting
Customer account, onboarding goal, and task registration validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: Replacing adjacent systems such as accounting or payroll
assumptions: User and organization data are available through company SSO.
Operators manage policy and classification criteria.
risks: Weak initial classification can cause incorrect processing.
Stale source data can reduce operational trust.
openQuestions: What defines urgent processing and approval?
What are the post-completion verification and reopen rules?
constraints: State, owner, and priority changes require audit logs.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.

acceptance.text: Required context, changes, ownership, and status are retained in an auditable record.
